item.title: Starting a gnome-shell extension
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Since I moved from UbunôÙtu to FeôÙdoôÙra earôÙly this year, I changed my deskôÙtop enôÙviôÙronôÙment from uniôÙty to Gnome ShelôÙl.

I must say it is a great exôÙpeôÙriôÙence. At the beôÙginôÙning I was thinkôÙing on what was betôÙter comôÙpared to uniôÙty (beôÙcause I did not like UniôÙty). ReôÙgardôÙing this comôÙparôÙiôÙson I found a more staôÙble and usôÙable winôÙdow manôÙagôÙer. I still preôÙfer someôÙthing simôÙpler perôÙhapôÙs, but it works good and it is nice.

Just out of cuôÙriosôÙiôÙty, one day I startôÙed manôÙagôÙing some exôÙtenôÙsionôÙs. Oh, by the way, you will probôÙaôÙbly be inôÙstalling some exôÙtenôÙsions for Gnome shelôÙl, some of them are useôÙful and some othôÙers make it more usôÙable. That drove me to the exôÙtenôÙsions deôÙvelôÙopôÙer API, which turned out to be quite simôÙple and inôÙterôÙestôÙing.

HavôÙing played a litôÙtle bit, I deôÙcidôÙed to code a (very) simôÙple exôÙtenôÙsion that does just one sinôÙgle thing. I called it "simôÙple-ôÙname" and it is availôÙable at: SimôÙple-ôÙName-ExôÙtenôÙsion

It reôÙmemôÙbers some litôÙtle deôÙtail I was missôÙing from some preôÙviôÙous winôÙdow manôÙagers that I have used along the years, which is to disôÙplay the userôÙname of the curôÙrentôÙly logged-in usôÙer.

I must say that, I code it just for the sake of learnôÙing and exôÙperôÙiôÙmentôÙing with and API and a bit of JavaScripôÙt. In adôÙdiôÙtion I found very inôÙterôÙestôÙing to see fronôÙt-end reôÙlatôÙed techôÙnoloôÙgies like JavaScript or CSS used in the deskôÙtop enôÙviôÙronôÙment (not that is someôÙthing new, but still funôÙny).

To sum up, I wantôÙed to highôÙlight that is inôÙterôÙestôÙing to learn new techôÙnoloôÙgies, even with litôÙtle and simôÙple steps like a new API, liôÙbrary, etc. It is not for the techôÙnolôÙoôÙgy itôÙself, but for the sake of learnôÙing :)
